site.title: THE O-CODEX
site.subtitle: A shared reference for the wandering company, gathered from rumor, memory, and hard-earned truth.

categories.label: Characters
categories.description: Allies, rivals, patrons, and dangerous names the party keeps hearing.

categories.label: Settlements
categories.description: Settlements, capitals, and crossroads worth remembering.

categories.label: Stories
categories.description: Threads the party can follow, revisit, or fear they have left too long.

categories.label: Manifests
categories.description: Relics, tools, and heirlooms with enough history to matter.

categories.label: Divions
categories.description: Orders, branches, or hidden structures waiting to be filled in.

categories.label: Summaries
categories.description: Session recaps, overviews, and high-level records for the party.

entries.name: Cooper Vale
entries.summary: A soft-spoken trail guide who remembers every road that once mattered.
entries.description: Cooper works as a guide for caravans moving between the western marsh roads and the hill forts inland. He speaks gently, notices everything, and becomes hard to fool once someone lies to him. Locals trust him with routes they would never write down, especially when the old roads begin shifting in bad weather.
entries.status: Usually available in Riverwatch between caravan seasons

details.Known For: Remembering abandoned roads and smuggler cuts
details.Party Hook: Can point the group toward hidden passes or forgotten shrines

entries.name: Mirelle Thorne
entries.summary: An exhausted noble archivist trying to keep dangerous records out of the wrong hands.
entries.description: Mirelle serves the Candle Bastion archive, where she catalogs disputed histories and sealed testimonies. She is precise in speech, patient with curious people, and visibly tense whenever court politics enter the room. She often hires capable outsiders when she needs something done quietly.

details.Useful Secret: Knows which family lines were erased from public records

entries.name: Brannic Salt
entries.summary: A scarred harbor broker who trades in rumors as often as cargo.
entries.description: Brannic controls a surprising amount of dockside traffic without ever claiming to be in charge of anything. He buys stolen maps, broken relics, and information from sailors too desperate to keep it. He smiles often, but only when he believes he already knows what someone will say next.

details.Leverage: Debt ledgers and private shipping manifests

entries.name: Riverwatch
entries.summary: A trade city of flooded stairs, rope bridges, and too many secrets in the lower docks.
entries.description: Riverwatch sits where the blackwater channels widen enough for real trade. Its upper wards are built from reclaimed fort stone, while the lower wards lean over the water in cramped timber layers. Caravans, barges, smugglers, mercenaries, and pilgrims all cross paths here, which makes the city wealthy and consistently one bad week away from chaos.

details.Adventure Use: Excellent base city for recurring NPCs and side quests

entries.name: Crownhaven
entries.summary: The inland capital where polished ceremonies hide brittle politics.
entries.description: Crownhaven is the ceremonial heart of the realm, though many border folk would argue it has forgotten how the realm actually lives. The city is elegant, heavily watched, and full of old institutions that still matter because everyone fears what would happen if they stopped. Public order is strong, but influence is the true currency.

details.Ruling Presence: High Court and its attached ministries
details.Street Reputation: Beautiful, expensive, and dangerous to misread
details.Story Use: Court intrigue, records research, elite rivals

entries.name: Hollowmere
entries.summary: A marsh settlement built around peat fires, old graves, and stubborn people.
entries.description: Hollowmere survives because the people there know the marsh better than anyone who would try to conquer it. Homes cluster on raised stone foundations, and every family seems to remember a ghost story tied to the nearby barrows. Visitors are tolerated as long as they respect the water, the dead, and local warnings.

details.Exports: Bog salt, reed cloth, medicinal moss
details.Threats: Sinkways, grave robbers, pale lights over the water

entries.title: The Moonlit Oath
entries.summary: Recover a missing oathblade before two feuding knightly lines start a private war.
entries.description: A ceremonial sword vanished on the night both houses were meant to renew a truce under moonlight. Each side insists the other staged the theft, and both are close to mobilizing retainers. The party can investigate the theft, expose the real culprit, or decide whether the truth is worth the blood it may spill anyway.

details.Primary Location: Crownhaven and the Moon Court estate roads
Possible Outcomes: Keep the blade hidden for a third faction

entries.title: The Bell Below the Mire
entries.summary: The marsh bells have started ringing underground, and no one agrees on why.
entries.description: Hollowmere fishers report hearing temple bells under the peat after sundown. The sound has drawn lights over the bog, strange footprints near old graves, and a sudden burst of grave robbery in nearby barrows. The party needs to learn whether something buried is waking, calling, or simply baiting the living closer.

details.Recommended Tone: Eerie investigation that can turn into a descent
Main Clues: Priests arguing over a forbidden burial rite

entries.title: The Golden Tax
entries.summary: A royal tax convoy has vanished, and too many people want it found for the wrong reason.
entries.description: A heavily guarded convoy carrying tax silver never arrived in Crownhaven. Officials want quiet recovery, dock brokers want the route, and local rebels want proof that the treasury is weaker than it appears. Following the trail means choosing who gets the truth and who gets a lie they can live with.

Complications: The guards may have staged the disappearance
Brannic Salt may already have one chest
The silver could fund relief for frontier towns

entries.name: Lantern Reliquary
entries.summary: A bronze field lantern that reveals old ward lines and hidden shrine marks.
entries.description: This squat lantern burns with pale oil light that does not behave like ordinary flame. When opened in darkness, it traces old protective symbols across stone, roots, and thresholds. Priests claim it was once used to inspect forgotten pilgrim roads after wars left the temples in ruin.

details.Practical Use: Helps locate hidden symbols, shrines, and warded passageways
details.Drawback: Its light can also reveal the holder's presence to certain spirits
details.Current Rumor: One was seen on sale in Riverwatch for far too little

entries.name: Ashen Signet
entries.summary: A ring of blackened silver that warms when old vows are spoken nearby.
entries.description: The Ashen Signet was forged for oathkeepers who needed warning when sworn promises were being twisted or broken. It does not reveal lies directly, but it reacts to binding language, compelled bargains, and hidden terms spoken within earshot. Many courts would pay well to keep such a thing private.

details.Complication: May react to ancient vows no one meant to disturb
details.Suggested Hook: Tie it to The Moonlit Oath or a hidden succession claim

entries.name: Gravesalt Flask
entries.summary: A stoppered iron flask that keeps blessed salt dry even in cursed wetlands.
entries.description: Priests of the old road shrines used these flasks while traveling through rain, marsh, and battlefield mud. A pinch of the preserved salt can strengthen funerary rites, harden a temporary ward, or expose where the dead have been disturbed recently. It looks humble, but people who know what it is rarely ignore it.

details.Rarity: Common among old shrine keepers, scarce elsewhere
details.Perfect For: Hollowmere stories, grave sites, and haunted roads
details.Maintenance: Needs to be refilled during proper rites
